$comment: Hand-authored events for the eastern_europe scenario. Timestamps use offset_hours relative to export time so the demo always feels fresh. Weights are signed in [-1, 1]; sign is from the perspective of the controlling faction (NATO for terr.lithuania/terr.poland_ne, RU for terr.kaliningrad/terr.belarus_w). See docs/intel.md.

events.headline: Vilnius rally backs increased defence spending
events.snippet: Tens of thousands gathered in Vilnius to back a parliament bill raising defence spending to 4% of GDP, citing concerns over Suwalki corridor security.

events.headline: Reservist call-up oversubscribed in Kaunas
events.snippet: Lithuanian defence ministry reports a 140% turnout for the spring reservist exercise. Analysts cite cross-border tension as a driver.

events.headline: Energy prices climb 11% on grid disconnect anxieties
events.snippet: Spot electricity prices in the Baltic states rose sharply this week amid uncertainty around BRELL synchronisation timelines.

events.headline: Coalition partner threatens walkout over fuel tax
events.snippet: A junior coalition party in Vilnius threatened to leave government over a proposed fuel-tax hike tied to the new defence budget.

events.headline: Two NATO armoured vehicles damaged in training accident
events.snippet: A column of Boxer IFVs from the multinational NATO battlegroup sustained damage during a night exercise near Pabrade. No casualties reported.

events.headline: Public opinion poll: 78% support hosting more allied troops
events.snippet: A nationwide poll found record support for permanent allied basing in Lithuania.

events.headline: Small farmers' protest blocks A1 outside Kaunas
events.snippet: A few hundred farmers blocked the Kaunas-Klaipeda highway over EU subsidy delays. Roads cleared after four hours.

events.headline: Bank of Lithuania trims growth outlook to 1.6%
events.snippet: Central bank cited weaker external demand and elevated defence imports.

events.headline: Independence anniversary draws record crowds
events.snippet: Cathedral Square in Vilnius saw the largest March 11 turnout in a decade.

events.headline: Cyber probe attributed to GRU disrupts railway scheduling briefly
events.snippet: Lithuanian Railways reported a 90-minute outage after a state-linked intrusion attempt.

events.headline: Justice minister survives narrow no-confidence vote
events.snippet: Vote split 71-69 in the Seimas; coalition holds, but unity is fraying.

events.headline: Defence procurement bill clears committee with 90% support
events.snippet: Cross-party support for the package signals strong public mood for hardening.

events.headline: Warsaw approves new armoured division standup near Suwalki
events.snippet: Polish MoD confirmed accelerated formation of the 1st Armoured Division HQ in Bialystok with full IOC by autumn.

events.headline: Polish K2 tank deliveries accelerate
events.snippet: Hyundai Rotem confirmed an early delivery of 24 K2 tanks ahead of contract schedule, mostly destined for the eastern flank.

events.headline: Farmers' protest near Bialystok blocks border traffic
events.snippet: Around 600 farmers blocked the S8 protesting Ukrainian grain imports. Police negotiating partial reopening.

events.headline: Inflation in NE voivodeships rebounds to 5.2% YoY
events.snippet: Lublin and Podlaskie reported the steepest jumps; food and energy costs cited.

events.headline: Prime minister visits 18th Mech Bde, pledges accelerated readiness
events.snippet: Visit to Bialystok included a public announcement of a 12% pay raise for combat units.

events.headline: Drone incursion downs power line near Sejny
events.snippet: Suspected Belarus-launched drone clipped a 110kV line. No casualties; outage restored in 8 hours.

events.headline: Truckers stage rolling slowdown at Kuznica crossing
events.snippet: Polish hauliers slowed border traffic to a crawl over EU permit changes.

events.headline: Public approval for NATO posture hits 86%
events.snippet: CBOS poll: 86% support permanent US basing in Poland; 74% back forward presence in NE region.

events.headline: Local mayor clashes with central government over land use
events.snippet: Augustow mayor publicly opposed expansion of training ground; standoff continues.

events.headline: Border-region tourism revenue down 22% YoY
events.snippet: Operators cite security perception and reduced cross-border traffic with Belarus.

events.headline: Volunteer territorial defence enrolment up 31%
events.snippet: WOT reports surge in NE voivodeships, citing recent drone incidents.

events.headline: Reports of fuel-depot fire at Chkalovsk airbase
events.snippet: Open-source imagery shows smoke plume consistent with a fuel-storage detonation. Russian sources blame 'technical incident'.

events.headline: EU sanctions tighten on Kaliningrad transit
events.snippet: New EU package restricts dual-use transit through Lithuanian rail corridor; oblast officials warn of supply pinch.

events.headline: Small protest in Kaliningrad over price of basic goods
events.snippet: Roughly 300 demonstrators in Pobedy Square; police dispersed the gathering within an hour.

events.headline: Governor reshuffle after critical Telegram leak
events.snippet: Two deputy governors removed after an internal memo on logistics shortfalls leaked online.

events.headline: State media broadcasts Baltic Fleet anniversary parade
events.snippet: Heavy state media coverage of fleet review; counter-narrative against Western pressure.

events.headline: Iskander unit relocates further inland after OSINT exposure
events.snippet: Geolocated imagery prompted a redeployment of a brigade-level Iskander battery.

events.headline: Rouble weakness pushes import inflation in oblast above 9%
events.snippet: Imported goods, dominant in Kaliningrad's consumer basket, have driven a sharp price spike.

events.headline: Quiet vigil for Ukrainian-born detainee broken up
events.snippet: Small civil-society vigil broken up; multiple detentions reported.

events.headline: State TV runs week-long 'Western containment' series
events.snippet: Media campaign aims to consolidate domestic support for the Baltic Fleet build-up.

events.headline: Su-30 makes emergency landing after engine fire
events.snippet: Crew safe; airframe written off. Fleet readiness hit by maintenance backlog.

events.headline: Anti-corruption arrests sweep oblast administration
events.snippet: FSB-led arrests target customs and procurement officials in a high-profile case.

events.headline: Mass protests in Grodno over conscription orders
events.snippet: Several thousand demonstrators gathered in central Grodno after fresh conscription notices targeting a wider age band.

events.headline: Local council walkouts spread across western Belarus
events.snippet: Three regional councils in Grodno Voblast suspended sittings after opposition deputies refused to ratify mobilisation directives.

events.headline: Belarus rouble weakens 6% after sanctions package
events.snippet: New EU/UK sanctions trigger sharp depreciation; queues form at currency exchanges.

events.headline: Reports of desertions from 6th Sep Motor Rifle Bde
events.snippet: Unverified Telegram channels report a wave of unauthorised absences from a brigade based near Grodno.

events.headline: State rally in Minsk projects unity message
events.snippet: Government-organised rally drew tens of thousands; messaging emphasised Union State solidarity.

events.headline: Sit-in at Brest railway station enters second day
events.snippet: Workers protest unpaid bonuses; police presence increases but situation remains static.

events.headline: Wholesale food prices climb 12% in western oblasts
events.snippet: Border-trade disruptions and rouble weakness combine to push prices higher.

events.headline: Equipment fire at western Belarus depot
events.snippet: OSINT analysts report a fire at an ammo depot near Lida; impact on readiness unclear.

events.headline: Independent unions report fresh crackdown
events.snippet: Several union leaders detained; activists relocate operations abroad.

events.headline: Diaspora-led 'silent walks' draw participation in Grodno
events.snippet: Quiet protest movement re-emerges; turnout modest but growing.

events.headline: State TV runs hour-long Union State special
events.snippet: Heavy-handed messaging trying to consolidate base; analysts skeptical of impact.
